drop_row_name: Keppel Data Centres | Keppel DC Johor 1

correction_log: merge_pending_verification → merge_confirmed: operator page confirms facility identity; drop row has null coords so no coord conflict to resolve.

rationale: Operator site (princetondg.com/locations/malaysia/) explicitly places JH1 at Sedenak Tech Park (STeP), Kulai, Johor — 128,000 m² campus, 200 MW, 4 buildings. v5.1 coord (1.6923, 103.4145) falls inside STeP's polygon (STeP centroid ~1.69°N 103.41°E). v6_review PeeringDB coord (1.6642, 103.5298) is ~15 km east of STeP, in the Kulai town area — inconsistent with the operator's published location and clearly geocoding-drifted. Merge is correct: keep v5.1 row with STeP coord, drop PeeringDB row, carry pdb_id into the note.
correction_log: merge_pending_verification → merge_confirmed: operator site places JH1 at Sedenak Tech Park, matching v5.1 coord and contradicting the PeeringDB-drifted v6_review coord.

keep_row_name: YTL Johor Data Center 1 (Kulai) || YTL Johor Data Center 2 (Kulai) || YTL Johor Data Center 3 (Kulai)
rationale: Three rows at (1.6206, 103.5216), (1.6216, 103.5216), (1.6226, 103.5216) — a perfect 0.001°-lat stagger on identical lon. That pattern is not how a real campus lays out. YTL Green DC Park publishes a single campus in Kulai on 111ha; if these are distinct buildings they need real per-building coords from satellite imagery. RECOMMENDATION: keep as separate rows but flag all three coord_confidence='unknown' until each building is individually verified, or collapse to one campus row keyed by 'YTL Green Data Center Park (Kulai)'. Deferring to human decision.

AIMS has multiple Cyberjaya buildings (CJ1, Block 2, possibly more). PeeringDB labels one 'CJ1 Cyberjaya'; v5.1 has 'Block 2'. These may or may not be the same building — AIMS uses both conventions. Without an authoritative AIMS facility list, flag for human review rather than auto-merge.

Evidence reversal: the two PeeringDB records have DIFFERENT addresses, so they are two distinct buildings — not a duplicate. PDB fac/3168 (AIMS CJ1 Cyberjaya) is at Jalan Cyber Point 4, Cyber 8, Cyberjaya 63000. PDB fac/13120 (AIMS Cyberjaya) is at Jalan Teknokrat 1/2, Cyber 3, Cyberjaya 63000. Cyber 3 and Cyber 8 are separate zones within Cyberjaya with different street grids. The earlier merge assumption (same facility, one generic + one named) was wrong. Keep both rows. Flag: the drop row (91 AIMS Cyberjaya, Cyber 3 / Jalan Teknokrat) is currently coord-less and needs geocoding as a separate gapfill pass — it plausibly corresponds to the historical Menara AIMS / Block 2 facility in Cyber 3, which overlaps with the review_entry at row_idx 9 (AIMS Cyberjaya Block 2). Recommend human checks whether row 9 and row 91 describe the same Cyber 3 building before row 91 is filled in.
correction_log: merge_pending_verification → not_duplicate_keep_both: PeeringDB records have distinct Cyberjaya addresses (Cyber 8 vs Cyber 3). Previous merge assumption reversed.

rationale: NTT's own newsroom confirms its Cyberjaya site is a campus with numbered buildings (CBJ1 through CBJ6); CBJ6 is a specific 2023 build (7 MW, 4,890 m²) that forms a 'combined facility' with CBJ5. The PeeringDB record fac/1902 (low ID, dating to early PDB era ~2010) labels just 'CBJ' and carries the campus address (43000 Jalan APEC, Cyberjaya) with null GPS — consistent with the original (CBJ1) building from when there was only one 'CBJ'. CBJ1 and CBJ6 are distinct physical buildings on the same campus, so the PDB 'CBJ' row should NOT be merged into CBJ6. Action: keep both rows. The v6_review 'NTT Cyberjaya Data Center (CBJ)' row effectively represents CBJ1 and needs coord-fill from the published campus address in a separate gapfill pass (and ideally a rename to 'NTT Cyberjaya CBJ1' to disambiguate).
correction_log: review → not_duplicate_keep_both: NTT campus has distinct numbered buildings CBJ1–CBJ6; the unnumbered 'CBJ' PDB record is the original CBJ1, not CBJ6.
